channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: The U.S. will resume Ukraine military aid and intelligence, as Kyiv approves ceasefire
item.description: The United States said it will resume sending military aid and intelligence to Ukraine, as Ukraine agreed to a Trump administration proposal for a monthlong ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis, Michele Kelemen, and Eleanor Beardsley, MPR News

item.title: Virginia’s Earle-Sears drops first ad confronting Dem opponent over migrant crime, border wall opposition
item.description: An ad from Virginia Lt. Gov. Winsome Earle-Sears plays clips of former Rep. Abigail Spanberger's comments, including her opposition to Trump's border wall, and news clippings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Buying Time
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Republicans pass bill to avert government shutdown
item.description: The House on Tuesday passed a funding bill to avert an end-of-the-week government shutdown, teeing up the measure for consideration in the Senate. The chamber cleared the continuing resolution (CR) in a largely party-line 217-213 vote, with just one Democrat — Rep.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: 2 senior judges, appointed by Repubicans, speak out about threats against federal judiciary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Two senior federal judges, both appointed by Republican presidents, spoke out Tuesday against threats of violence and impeachment against their colleagues in the judiciary. “Threats against judges are threats against constitutional government.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump selects a new Tesla on White House driveway to show support for Elon Musk
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump shopped for a new Tesla on the White House driveway on Tuesday, selecting a shiny red sedan to show his support for Elon Musk 's electric vehicle company as it faces blowback because of his work to advance the president's political agenda and downsize the fed
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump halts doubling of tariffs on Canadian metals, after Ontario suspends electricity price hikes
item.description: The White House trade adviser Peter Navarro said the U.S. president pulled back on his doubling of steel and aluminum tariffs, even as the federal government still plans to place a 25 percent tariff on all steel and aluminum imports starting Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: DOGE protesters rally outside key department after employees are told not to report to work Wednesday
item.description: Protesters against DOGE rallied outside the Education Department and held a moment of appreciation and applause for the federal agency rumored to be the next on the chopping block.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House: Rubio reserves the right to revoke green card or visa of anti-Israel activist Mahmoud Khalil
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said Secretary of State Marco Rubio can revoke Mahmoud Khalil's green card out of national security concern.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Risch, GOP lawmakers demand Olympic committee comply with Trump order to ban trans athletes in women's sports
item.description: More than two dozen Senate and House Republicans demand the International Olympics Committee align with President Donald Trump's executive order banning trans athletes from women's sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon clips civilian employees' credit cards in line with DOGE cuts
item.description: The Trump administration has drawn down the spending limit on government-issued travel and purchase cards for federal civilian employees to just one dollar.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans ask party to keep clean energy credits
item.description: The Republicans who signed the letter have not ruled out voting against a continuing resolution to fund the government.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Vance gives House GOP closing pitch on Trump-backed funding bill ahead of vote
item.description: Vice President Vance delivered a closing pitch in favor of Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) government funding bill during the House GOP’s private conference meeting Tuesday, hours before the lower chamber is scheduled to vote on the measure.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Overpaid by Social Security? You’ll now owe 100% of the repayment
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – If you accidentally got overpaid through your Social Security benefits, you’ll have to pay 100% of it back starting in late March, the agency announced on Monday. The overpayment withholding rate was previously 10%.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Trump says he’s buying ‘brand new’ Tesla to support Musk
item.description: President Donald Trump said he would be buying a “brand new” Tesla to support his senior adviser Elon Musk, as the stock market shows shares of the electric car company tumbling.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: How reliable is the government’s economic data? Under Trump, there are real concerns
item.description: The disbanding of committees that consulted on government data — and comments from a senior official about changing how GDP is calculated — are raising alarm about the reliability of government data.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley, MPR News

item.title: Senate GOP frets as Wall Street takes dip over tariffs, talk of recession
item.description: Senate Republicans expressed new worries about the economy Monday after Wall Street had one of its worst days in years amid a trade war and remarks from President Trump that did not rule out the possibility of a recession.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida seeks easier voting access for hurricane-displaced citizens
item.description: Last year's hurricanes displaced thousands of Floridians, turning their lives upside down and making it even harder for some to vote during a crucial election year. Now, a Pinellas County state lawmaker wants to make it easier for people to cast their ballots after a storm.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Lori Chavez-DeRemer as Labor secretary
item.description: The Senate on Monday confirmed former Rep. Lori Chavez-DeRemer (R-Ore.) to lead the Department of Labor, cementing another of President Trump’s nominees in bipartisan fashion. Senators voted 67 to 32, with 17 Democrats voted with most Republicans present. Sens. Rand Paul (R-Ky.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Race for NYC mayor: Public safety and Cuomo defense money
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Candidates in the race for New York City mayor continue to take aim at former Gov. Andrew Cuomo's public safety agenda and tax dollars being spent on his legal troubles.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Florida bill would require some elementary school students to learn cursive
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — A new Florida bill would require some elementary school students to learn cursive writing. The bill, HB 921, would require all public schools to provide cursive writing instruction to students in grades 2 through 5.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Protestors dispute Vance’s description of clash on street over Ukraine
item.description: In a post on X, Vance said he was confronted by pro-Ukraine protestors while he was with his 3-year-old daughter on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota lawmakers consider allowing heavier ATVs
item.description: The ATV industry says consumer preferences are shifting toward larger side-by-side vehicles. Some environmental groups question whether heavier vehicles could cause more trail damage.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Renewed debate sparks discussion on 'My Safe Florida Home' program
item.description: Governor Ron DeSantis wants more Floridians to benefit from the "My Safe Florida Home" program, which provides storm-hardening home improvements for homeowners.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas homeless camps just move when outreach comes, but proposal might change that
item.description: When homeless camps get out of control, local governments send teams to clean up. That can be a disaster, costing people what few possessions they have. But trash and unsanitary conditions are becoming a bigger priority as residents go to elected officials demanding action.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Price fixing on essential goods in Nevada targeted, but businesses are not on board
item.description: A bill that targets fraudulent and deceptive actions that try to manipulate prices of essential goods and services prompted a backlash from the business community on Wednesday in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada Democrats react to Trump's speech; state Republicans applaud his vision
item.description: Democrats who represent Nevada in Washington, D.C., condemned what they see as a failure by President Donald Trump to lower prices for Nevada families, while state Republicans applauded his leadership the morning after his address to a joint session of Congress Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
